| AlternativelyKnownAs | BSOAlternativeIdentity | StatusReport | 0..* | Alternate Name for the BSO. If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |
| BattlespaceIIE | BattlespaceIIE | StatusReport | 1..1 |  |

It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). ### 16. AirTaskingOrder

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 16 |
| Name | AirTaskingOrder |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::Product::APP-11::AirTaskingOrder::AirTaskingOrder |
| Type | `Type:<Class>` |
| Description | Message identifier (Name):ATO (AIR TASKING ORDER) Related Documents:AJP-1 Allied Joint Doctrine AJP-3 Allied Joint Doctrine for the Conduct of Operations AJP-3.3 Allied Joint Doctrine for Air and Space Operations Purpose:The ATO is used to task offensive, defensive and support missions including surveillance and control assets in order to conduct both joint and single service air operations. Sponsor:MCASB AIR OPERATIONS WORKING GROUP (AOWG) Notes:The ATO can be used for both pre-planned and immediate tasking; however, for the battle management additional message formats may be used. Status:Published [APP-11-D-1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_SWy64KDHEeqz4qMNCq9PtQ |

If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

### 38. BMAreaAtRisk

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 38 |
| Name | BMAreaAtRisk |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Feature::BallisticMissile::BMAreaAtRisk |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | An Area at Risk is a geographical location or area that Intelligence staff assess to be at risk of BM attack during a period of interest within an OPFOR COA. The assessment will be based on assets in the JPCAL that: are within range of BMOAs; meet the OPFOR targeting strategy; are locatable by the OPFOR targeting capability; and that the OPFOR perceives to be critical to NATO objectives, vulnerable to attack and have low recuperability. -- TBMD Intelligence Requirements, Version 2, 19 July 2013Areas at Risk: List of areas at risk of ballistic missile (BM) attack provided as a separate product from the Opposing Force BM Course of Action (OPFOR BM COA).[BMD-ADD] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_oYNfEKpIEeq0kau1c3OzlQ |

If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| AnalystComment | String | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..1 | Comment on the product. The Comment field will identify analyst comments regarding the implications of the information and other assessments. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

| AlternativelyKnownAs | BSOAlternativeIdentity | StatusReport | 0..* | Alternate Name for the BSO. If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| AnalystComment | String | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..1 | Comment on the product. The Comment field will identify analyst comments regarding the implications of the information and other assessments. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |
| BattlespaceIIE | BattlespaceIIE | StatusReport | 1..1 |  |

### 49. BMOAActivityComparison

|---|---|
| # | 49 |
| Name | BMOAActivityComparison |

| Description | A Comparison of Expected Opposing Force Ballistic Missile Course of Action Versus Activity records the comparison of OPFOR BM COA components (BM types, trajectory types, BM numbers, launch and impact points, target types, warhead types) for each BMOA based on observed BM firing events against a selected expected OPFOR BM COA. | Field | Value |

| # | 50 |
| Name | BMOAStatusReport |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Facility::BallisticMissile::BMOAStatusReport |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | A Ballistic Missile Operating Area (BMOA) is a geographical region from which BM threats can be launched. Each BMOA may be associated with multiple threats and threat types. -- TBMD Intelligence Requirements, Version 2.0, 19 July 2013 |

If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

### 51. BMOPFORComparisonRecord

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 51 |
| Name | BMOPFORComparisonRecord |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::JIPOE::Step3ThreatIntegration::BallisticMissile::COAActivityComparison::BMOPFORComparisonRecord |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | A Comparison of Expected Opposing Force Ballistic Missile Course of Action Versus Activity records the comparison of OPFOR BM COA components (BM types, trajectory types, BM numbers, launch and impact points, target types, warhead types) for each BMOA based on observed BM firing events against a selected expected OPFOR BM COA. [BMD-ADD] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_GScXoDonEeu_8e-rNS-THA |

### 52. BMORBAT

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 52 |
| Name | BMORBAT |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::ORBAT::BallisticMissile::BMORBAT |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | An OrganisationStructure that provides the identification, strength, command structure, and disposition of the personnel, units, and equipment of any military force. Remarks: Although sometimes used in other ways, this term most frequently applies to the grouping of forces for a specific campaign. [MIM-5.1]An Order of Battle (ORBAT) is is a catalogue listing the entities (force elements, military units and non-military elements) potentially available for operations with the ability to structure these entities into an organisational hierarchy. [TOPFAS CDM] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_JavYsAe7EeuvROhoDtf-HQ |

### 60. BallisticMissile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 60 |
| Name | BallisticMissile |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Materiel::Equipment::BallisticMissile::BallisticMissile |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | The Ballistic Type Details data element represents the parameters for a ballistic missile type as an overall system, for example range and accuracy. A Ballistic Missile Type represents the static intelligence information held on a type of ballistic missile.BM Type Details: Additional characteristics of a ballistic missile (BM) type not stored in the opposing force (OPFOR) BM order of battle (ORBAT). Required for detailed analysis and modelling. [BMD-ADD] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_3G8psPjmEemscYqpTO3mmA |

### 61. BallisticMissileHolding

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 61 |
| Name | BallisticMissileHolding |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Actor::Organisation::Unit::BallisticMissile::BallisticMissileHolding |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | <p A Ballistic Missile (BM) Holding represents an intelligence assessment of a collection of BMs belonging to an OPFOR BM ORBAT. Equipment may be tracked without knowing the force to which is belongs. Where an assessment of ownership has been made it represents a BM Force's inventory of such weapons. [BMD-ADD]</p <p NB: The three types of Holding represent actual equipment. Equipment may be tracked without knowing the force to which is belongs (represented by the multiplicity with respect to OPFOR BM Force being 0 or 1). Where an assessment of ownership has been made it represents a BM Forces Holdings. That is its inventory of key BM equipment and weapons; [BMD-LDM]</p |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_vJFdSpP7EeyQl-341BO9AA |

| AlternativelyKnownAs | BSOAlternativeIdentity | StatusReport | 0..* | Alternate Name for the BSO. If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

| Address | PhysicalAddress | StatusReport | 0..1 |  |
| AlternativelyKnownAs | BSOAlternativeIdentity | StatusReport | 0..* | Alternate Name for the BSO. If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| AnalystComment | String | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..1 | Comment on the product. The Comment field will identify analyst comments regarding the implications of the information and other assessments. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| BallisticMissileEquipment | BallisticMissile | BallisticMissileTECHINTStatusReport | 0..1 |  |
| BallisticMissileTECHINT | BallisticMissileTECHINT | BallisticMissileTECHINTStatusReport | 1..1 |  |
| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |
| BattlespaceIIE | BattlespaceIIE | StatusReport | 1..1 |  |
| CapableOfDepressedTrajectory | Boolean | BallisticMissileTECHINTStatusReport | 0..1 | Whether the type of ballistic missile (BM) is capable of being launched on a depressed trajectory |

### 65. BasicEncyclopediaNumberType

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 65 |
| Name | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::Datatypes::BasicEncyclopediaNumberType |
| Type | `<DataType>` |
| Description | Target identifiers are a unique alphanumeric convention that can be assigned to a range of entities including facilities, individuals, equipment, organizations, and virtual structures. Target identifiers are included in the widely recognized basic encyclopedia number (BEN) system. -- USA Air Force Doctrine Publication 3-60, Targeting From USAF INTELLIGENCE TARGETING GUIDE, AIR FORCE PAMPHLET 14- 210 Intelligence, 1 FEBRUARY 1998:"1.7.3.1 Basic Encyclopedia (BE) Numbers (BEN). The ten- character BE number has two parts: the World Aeronautical Chart (WAC) number, which consists of four characters; and the installation number, which consists of either six numeric characters, one alpha and five numeric characters, or two alpha and four numeric characters. A BE number may be used as follows:1.7.3.1.1. Standard BE numbers. Most installations in the Automated Installation Intelli-gence File have a BE number with a zero in the fifth character- position. Standard BE numbers are assigned sequentially by the producer. The exceptions are airfields and electronic sites.1.7.3.1.2. Non- DIA Produced BE Numbers. An installation discovered by non-DIA ele-ments and reported for inclusion in the Modernized Integrated Data Base (MIDB) is identified by a BE number that carries alpha characters in the fifth and sixth character positions. These characters represent the exploitation element that assigned the number and controls it.1.7.3.1.3. Electronic BE Numbers. The BE numbers for a non- communication electronic installation consists of the WAC number, with an "E" in the fifth character position, and a five- digit EOB site number.1.7.3.1.4. Fictitious BE Numbers. The BE number for a fictitious (projected) installation has an "F" in the fifth character position. This is useful for exercise databases or for unclassified exercise scenarios.1.7.3.2. Functional Classification Codes. Each installation is classified or categorized to reflect products and military activity supported, etc. They are categorized by a five- digit code, as fol-lows:1. 7.3.2.1. The first digit gives the function in nine major categories. The other four digits show functions within the group. From left to right, each one describes the function or capa-bility of the installation more specifically. A code is assigned to each fixed facility that has some significance. The functional code for a mobile system (e. g., SAM, AAA, GCI, etc.) is assigned to the specific area that the system supports or where the activity is located (rather than the equipment itself). See DIAM 65- 3- 1 for these functional classification codes, com-monly known as category codes.EXAMPLE: 80052 Airfield, fighter base, primary mission is support of ground attack aircraft.1.7.3.3. Installation Names. Because of the many types of installations, coupled with the various name forms and component parts, a specific procedure is used to select and apply installation names. There are seven name components used to identify installations. These components appear in the following order: place name, functional name, distinguishing descriptive terms, proper name, honorary name, underground designation, and installation alpha or numerical desig-nators. However, not all of these names may appear on one installation. (For standard abbrevia-tions and procedures used, see the Target Intelligence Handbook.) CIA and some other sources of installation name do not always use the standard DoD naming scheme; be careful in trying to cor-relate installations based only on the name. The same applies to place names.1.7.3.4. Installation Coordinates. Latitude and longitude coordinates represent the fourth stan-dard element for installation identification. They are reference coordinates only and they are selected by approximating the center of mass for an installation. For airfields, the center of the runway or intersection of major runways is selected." |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_zScW8J-UEeyOTJlRnSKV0g |

| OrganisationalNode | String |  | 0..1 | Organisational Node = It can be an headquarters or other organisation.It provides a logical structuring of Users, functionality and information, and may be independent of the physical node structure.It may be accessed locally or across the wide-area network.It have the flexibility to fully operate in a disconnected environment, without connectivity with other Nodes and to synchronise data when the connectivity between Nodes becomes available -- Spiral 1 Training Material |

This is currently the richest POC definition from amongst the available standards. The name of the point of contact at the organisation that produced the product. [IPIWG-4.20] Product Point Of Contact. POC information should identify the individual or office with subject matter knowledge of the product, which may be different than the individual or office uploading the product. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| Producer | LibraryCardCommand |  | 1..1 | The entity primarily responsible for making the content of the resource. [IPIWG-4.20] The Producer of the product. The Producer attribute is to indicate the organisation/section that is responsible for the creation of the content of the product/report, which may be different than the organisation uploading the product. Users are not authorised to upload products with a Producer value different from their own organisation unless they are authorised by the actual producer to upload products on its behalf (e.g. SHAPE IRM uploads on behalf of Nations). [IFAS1-SOP] |

### 80. BombThreatWarning

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 80 |
| Name | BombThreatWarning |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::Product::APP-11::BombThreatWarning::BombThreatWarning |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | Message identifier (Name):BOMBWARN (BOMB THREAT WARNING) Related Documents:STANAG 2434 Purpose:The BOMBWARN is used to provide an immediate warning of the threat of an imminent bomb attack or detection of a suspected explosive device. Sponsor:NSA (ARMY) LAND OPERATIONS WORKING GROUP (LOWG) Notes:The BOMBWARN is by definition an alert measure. The results of a bomb attack carried out as part of terrorist or civil unrest activities are to be reported by the INCREP. Status:Published [APP-11-D-1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_SXKHQKDHEeqz4qMNCq9PtQ |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | ProductIIE | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

### 84. BusinessProcess

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 84 |
| Name | BusinessProcess |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::BusinessProcess::BusinessProcess |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | Choreography Instance represents an instantiation of a particular choreography or distributed workflow (for example: RFI, Disseminate IIE, ISR Request, Exploitation Task, etc.). It stores state pertinent to that workflow instance. Workflow progress is supported by the Choreography Task Message which accumulate on the Choreography instance in the manner of a ledger. Each choreography has a set of states through which it progresses. When in a particular state the control lies with certain roles. Only those roles can progress the state to the next state. State is advanced by participants sending messages. These messages should not be confused with the state. The messages communicate between participants that some event has occurred. The state of the choreography is then updated in response to, and as function of, that event |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_smuiYKVwEeq0iP2zpFZIvA |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 308/800 -->
<!-- Original visible header retained as metadata: NAF 4.0 L7 Information Model; Data Dictionary Version 1.1; original data dictionary page marker 260 / 1798 -->

### 116. CollectionExploitationPlan

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 116 |
| Name | CollectionExploitationPlan |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::IRMCM::CM::COM::CXP::CollectionExploitationPlan |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | collection and exploitation plan (CXP) A plan that provides detail of the tasks assigned to specific JISR capabilities, incl. PED to meet the formations JISR collection and exploitation requirements. The CXP is based upon CRs and direction from the JCMB articulated through the CTL. [APP-11, 2015] NATO Agreed.A collection and exploitation plan provides details of the tasks assigned to subordinate JISR collectors, exploiters, and units to meet JISR collection and exploitation requirements based upon essential elements of information (EEIs) that reflect intelligence and/or operational needs. This plan normally contains one days worth of JISR collection and exploitation tasks and is developed and managed by the collection management staff at each echelon on a daily basis. [AJP-2.7 Ed A] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_esVX_UEBEeqncIiWJGbPag |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 379/800 -->
<!-- Original visible header retained as metadata: NAF 4.0 L7 Information Model; Data Dictionary Version 1.1; original data dictionary page marker 331 / 1798 -->

### 118. CollectionRequirementList

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 118 |
| Name | CollectionRequirementList |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::IRMCM::CM::CRM::CollectionRequirementList |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | Collection Requirements List (CRL) A list of all prioritized JISR collection and PED requirements, including those that may be fulfilled by a formations JISR capabilities as well as requirements unable to be fulfilled by owned capability. [APP-11, 2015] NATO Agreed A collection requirement List is a list of unfulfilled ISR collection and exploitation requirements communicated from lower echelons as ISR requests for coordination. [MJ2 BRUC] If dedicated assets are not available collection requirements (CRs) are collated into a collection requirement list (CRL), which is then prioritized by the Joint Collection Management Board (JCMB) into the CTL for collection by either higher or adjacent headquarters. [AJP-2.1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_vihO8KiuEeqj-Pktwv_oDw |

### 121. CollectionTaskList

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 121 |
| Name | CollectionTaskList |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::IRMCM::CM::CRM::CollectionTaskList |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | The CTL is the authoritative collection management document for the theatre (AJP-2.7) The CTL may be published and disseminated on a daily basis in accordance with the operation and/or exercise battle rhythm (AJP-2.7) Collection task list (CTL) A list of prioritised JISR collection and PED requirements, developed from the CRL, which are allocated to JISR capabilities of own or subordinated formations. The CTL will be approved by the Joint Collection Management Board (JCMB). [APP-11, 2015] NATO Agreed. The CTL is consistent with the joint forces commanders overall mission priorities and the theatre collection priorities. Furthermore, the CTL provides a list of approved and prioritized JISR CRs, JISR tasks and as required dynamic re-tasking priorities. [AJP-2.7] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_o87iYKirEeqj-Pktwv_oDw |

| Description | Message identifier (Name):ASSESSREP (COMMANDERS ASSESSMENT REPORT) Related Documents:BI-SC REPORTING DIRECTIVE, VOL. III STANAG 2434 Purpose:THE ASSESSREP IS USED TO ADVISE SUPERIOR COMMANDERS OF THE SITUATION/OPERATIONS IN THE REPORTING COMMANDER'S AREA OF CONCERN, HIS ASSESSMENT OF THE OVERALL SITUATION, AND HIS INTENDED OR RECOMMENDED ACTIONS BASED ON THAT ASSESSMENT. Sponsor:NSA (ARMY) LAND OPERATIONS WORKING GROUP (LOWG) Notes:none Status:Published [APP-11-D-1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_SWvQgKDHEeqz4qMNCq9PtQ |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | ProductIIE | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

### 130. CommonCaptureReport

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 130 |
| Name | CommonCaptureReport |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::Product::APP-11::CommonCaptureReport::CommonCaptureReport |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | Message identifier (Name):COMCAPREP (COMMON CAPTURE REPORT) Related Documents:AINTP-3 STANAG 2044 STANAG 2084 Purpose:The COMCAPREP is used to alert the intelligence organization to the availability of a potential source of information immediately following the capture of prisoners of war (PW), and/or seizure of equipment or documents. Sponsor:NSA (JSB) JOINT INTELLIGENCE WORKING GROUP (JINTWG) Notes:The completion of the COMCAPREP and its early transmission to the nearest IU is of great importance to the exploitation of the intelligence potential of a prisoner of war. Status:Published [APP-11-D-1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_SXVtcKDHEeqz4qMNCq9PtQ |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 407/800 -->
<!-- Original visible header retained as metadata: NAF 4.0 L7 Information Model; Data Dictionary Version 1.1; original data dictionary page marker 359 / 1798 -->

| BPs | BusinessProcess | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..* |  |
| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | ProductIIE | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

### 132. CommsEmitter

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 132 |
| Name | CommsEmitter |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Materiel::Equipment::ElectronicWarfare::CommsEmitter |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | Communication Emitter specific characterstics are added to the Emittter Base Class for for CENOT and Signal Type. Sets containing different information about the comms signal are the defined for the emitter which are then combined to define Communication Modes. The Communication Modes can then be referenced from Modelines one to one for simple cases when more complex behaviour needs to be expressed, multiple modes can be combined sequentially or in parallel. [AEWP-01] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_g3uBUCTXEeuBN9AJjV_-mA |

### 142. Confidence

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 142 |
| Name | Confidence |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Metadata::Confidence |
| Type | `<Enumeration>` |
| Description | Confidence and probability. Throughout interpretation and all-source fusion, the analyst should attempt to find confirming information or intelligence. The degree of corroboration should enable levels of confidence to be expressed. The term 'confirmed' is rarely used in assessments given the nature of intelligence projecting forward in time. [AJP-2.1] High: Good quality of information, evidence from multiple collection capabilities, possible to make a clear judgment. Moderate: Evidence is open to a number of interpretations, or is credible and plausible but lacks correlation. Low: Fragmentary information, or from collection capabilities of dubious reliability. [AJP-2.1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_GFKckPv0EemNtI3vTXwM_w |

### 148. ControlFeature

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 148 |
| Name | ControlFeature |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Feature::ControlFeature |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | <p>A non-tangible Feature of military interest that is administratively specified, may be represented by a geometric figure, and is associated with the conduct of military operations.</p><p>Remarks:</p><p>A ControlFeature is an abstract object created or assigned by military authorities for the purposes of planning and coordination, especially in operational areas. It is a non-permanent point (e.g., start point for a road move, or a reserved demolition), line (e.g., a Main supply route or No fire line), area (e.g., a Slow-go area), or volume (e.g., an air corridor) that may be overlaid on a map. A ControlFeature would normally be drawn on a map overlay, traced, or superimposed onto digitised map data and assigned a descriptive title, symbol, or name (e.g., Line of departure, Corps boundary). [MIM-5.1].</p><p></p><p></p> |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_agHIUK6TEeyhqvYn5rpp5g |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 440/800 -->
<!-- Original visible header retained as metadata: NAF 4.0 L7 Information Model; Data Dictionary Version 1.1; original data dictionary page marker 392 / 1798 -->

| Description | Message identifier (Name):CISUPINTREP (COUNTER-INTELLIGENCE AND SECURITY SUPPLEMENTARY REPORT) Related Documents:STANAG 2511 Purpose:THE CISUPINTREP IS USED TO PROVIDE ALL ADDRESSEES WITH A COMPREHENSIVE REVIEW OF ALL COUNTER-INTELLIGENCE (CI) DATA COLLECTED OVER AN EXTENDED PERIOD OF TIME INCLUDING AN ASSESSMENT OF TRENDS IN THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE CI SITUATION. Sponsor:NSA (JSB) JOINT INTELLIGENCE WORKING GROUP (JINTWG) Notes:none Status:Published [APP-11-D-1] |

### 158. CounterMeasure

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 158 |
| Name | CounterMeasure |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Materiel::Equipment::BallisticMissile::CounterMeasure |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | The Stage, Decoy, Countermeasure and Re-entry Vehicle data elements represent the parameters for subcomponents of ballistic missiles since these are sometimes common to different missiles, variants or configurations. The Re-entry Vehicle data element includes warhead informationA Countermeasure specifies a type of payload carried by ballistic missile (BM) re-entry vehicles (RVs) to counter interception.[BMD-ADD] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_sN7QUPinEemkcIoqyiQteg |

### 160. CourseOfAction

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 160 |
| Name | CourseOfAction |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::Plan::CourseOfAction |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | course of action / mode d'action COA In the estimate process, an option that will accomplish or contribute to the accomplishment of a mission or task, and from which a detailed plan is developed. 2002.05.29 [AAP-6]In the estimate process, Course of Action (COA) is an option that will accomplish or contribute to the accomplishment of a mission or task, and from which a detailed plan is developed. Although the name Course of Action refers to actions for historical reasons, a COA in the Comprehensive Approach includes more than just the actions. Planning objects describing the aimed changes in the system such as the End State, Objectives and Effects are part of a COA. Strategic Options are similar to COAs but are aimed at strategic level. - Operations Planning Core Domain Model v1.1 2012 |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_N0-toKf7Eeq0mqb5FoCn4g |

### 161. Credibility

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 161 |
| Name | Credibility |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Metadata::Credibility |
| Type | `<Enumeration>` |
| Description | Evaluation allocates an alphanumeric rating to each piece of information or intelligence indicating the degree of assurance, which may be placed upon it. The evaluation rating is based partly on the subjective judgment of the evaluator, and, in the case of information produced by a sensor, on knowledge of the accuracy of the particular sensor system.69 Reliability and credibility should be considered independently of each other to ensure that the rating allocated to the reliability of the collection capability does not influence the rating given to the credibility of the information, or vice versa. [AJP-2.1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_520a0294faf74a4baa8f182753418b26 |

#### INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Metadata::Credibility - Owned Enumeration Literals

| Name | Short Name | Value |
|---|---|---|
| 1 |  | Confirmed |
| 2 |  | Probable |
| 3 |  | Possible |

| Description | The Stage, Decoy, Countermeasure and Re-entry Vehicle data elements represent the parameters for subcomponents of ballistic missiles since these are sometimes common to different missiles, variants or configurations. The Re-entry Vehicle data element includes warhead informationA Decoy specifies a type of payload carried by ballistic missiles (BMs) to confuse BMD systems about the location of re-entry vehicles.[BMD-ADD] |

| AnalystComment | String | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..1 | Comment on the product. The Comment field will identify analyst comments regarding the implications of the information and other assessments. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| BPs | BusinessProcess | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..* |  |
| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | ProductIIE | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

| Description | Message identifier (Name):DIR (DYNAMIC INTELLIGENCE REPORT) Related Documents:AD 80-70 MC 516 Purpose:The DIR (Dynamic Intelligence Report) is used for the dissemination of TBM (Theatre Ballistic Missile) threat data updates. Sponsor:ACO Notes:none Status:Published [APP-11-D-1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_SXYJsKDHEeqz4qMNCq9PtQ |

| BPs | BusinessProcess | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..* |  |
| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | ProductIIE | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

### 197. EOBEmitter

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 197 |
| Name | EOBEmitter |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Materiel::Equipment::ElectronicWarfare::EOBEmitter |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | An emitter is a base class which contains characteristics common to all emitters (NATO Spot Number, Name, Manufacturer and Primary Function Code). This base class is then extended into specific classes for:Radar, Communication and EO/IR Emitters with characteristics which are relevant only to that type of emitter (e.g. ELNOT for RadarEmitter, CENOT fo CommsEmitter). [AEWP-01] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_-KUAUCWVEeu29fAKOsasmA |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

### 207. EOIREmitter

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 207 |
| Name | EOIREmitter |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Materiel::Equipment::ElectronicWarfare::EOIREmitter |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | EO/IR Emitters contain specific characterstics for the EOIR System Type which specificy the type (Laser, Lamp, LED etc) and Nominal Ocular Hazard Distance (NOHD). These are added to those inherited from the Emittter Base Class. Sets containing different information about the EO/IR signal are the defined for the emitter which are then combined to define EOIR Modes. The EOIR Modes can then be referenced from Modelines one to one for simple cases when more complex behaviour needs to be expressed, multiple modes can be combined sequentially or in parallel. [AEWP-01] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_g0_dcCTXEeuBN9AJjV_-mA |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 555/800 -->

### 209. EOIREmitterTECHINT

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 209 |
| Name | EOIREmitterTECHINT |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Materiel::Equipment::ElectronicWarfare::EOIREmitterTECHINT |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | EO/IR Emitters contain specific characterstics for the EOIR System Type which specificy the type (Laser, Lamp, LED etc) and Nominal Ocular Hazard Distance (NOHD). These are added to those inherited from the Emittter Base Class. Sets containing different information about the EO/IR signal are the defined for the emitter which are then combined to define EOIR Modes. The EOIR Modes can then be referenced from Modelines one to one for simple cases when more complex behaviour needs to be expressed, multiple modes can be combined sequentially or in parallel. [AEWP-01] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_0A4a0CToEeu7ZKvHyIs6JQ |

Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

### 212. EWFacility

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 212 |
| Name | EWFacility |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Facility::ElectronicWarfare::EWFacility |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | A site entity represents a specific installation which can contain one or more emitters. An example of this would be an Airbase. A facility enity represents a specific emtter installation within that site, for example a radar tower at the Airbase.The Facility provides more fidelity to the individual elements which constitute the Site site. There is a parent-child relation between Site and Facilities. The Facilities inherit the AreaCode, GeoEntity and UserCountry of the parent Site as these will be common for all the facilities within the Site. They can however overwrite the LocationInfo and specify their own GeoCoordinatesGroup which allows Facilities to be precisely located within the containing site Equipment and/or Platforms can also be associated to a site. For instance a particular type of aircraft located could be located at the Airbase (e.g. SU-34). An example of a platform associated at the site could be a mobile Surface To Air missile system permanently located at the site to fulfil an Air Defence function. [AEWP-01] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_uuAtAJS0EeyQl-341BO9AA |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 572/800 -->
<!-- Original visible header retained as metadata: NAF 4.0 L7 Information Model; Data Dictionary Version 1.1; original data dictionary page marker 524 / 1798 -->

### 214. EWSite

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 214 |
| Name | EWSite |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Facility::ElectronicWarfare::EWSite |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | A site entity represents a specific installation which can contain one or more emitters. An example of this would be an Airbase. A facility enity represents a specific emtter installation within that site, for example a radar tower at the Airbase.The Facility provides more fidelity to the individual elements which constitute the Site site. There is a parent-child relation between Site and Facilities. The Facilities inherit the AreaCode, GeoEntity and UserCountry of the parent Site as these will be common for all the facilities within the Site. They can however overwrite the LocationInfo and specify their own GeoCoordinatesGroup which allows Facilities to be precisely located within the containing site Equipment and/or Platforms can also be associated to a site. For instance a particular type of aircraft located could be located at the Airbase (e.g. SU-34). An example of a platform associated at the site could be a mobile Surface To Air missile system permanently located at the site to fulfil an Air Defence function. [AEWP-01] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_uuAs-pS0EeyQl-341BO9AA |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 581/800 -->
<!-- Original visible header retained as metadata: NAF 4.0 L7 Information Model; Data Dictionary Version 1.1; original data dictionary page marker 533 / 1798 -->

| Address | PhysicalAddress | StatusReport | 0..1 |  |
| AlternativelyKnownAs | BSOAlternativeIdentity | StatusReport | 0..* | Alternate Name for the BSO. If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| AnalystComment | String | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..1 | Comment on the product. The Comment field will identify analyst comments regarding the implications of the information and other assessments. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| BPs | BusinessProcess | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..* |  |
| BSIIEAttributes | BattlespaceAttribute | StatusReport | 0..* |  |
| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |
| BattlespaceIIE | BattlespaceIIE | StatusReport | 1..1 |  |

| Description | Message identifier (Name):ENSITREP (ENEMY LAND FORCES SITUATION REPORT) Related Documents:STANAG 2022 STANAG 2434 Purpose:The ENSITREP is used to report and inform on the enemy forces situation, to include: locations, activities, boundaries, status, order of battle (ORBAT) and subordination of units/formations. Sponsor:NSA (ARMY) LAND OPERATIONS WORKING GROUP (LOWG) Notes:none Status:Published [APP-11-D-1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_SXdCMKDHEeqz4qMNCq9PtQ |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | ProductIIE | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

### 227. Equipment

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 227 |
| Name | Equipment |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Materiel::Equipment::Equipment |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | <p><span style="font-size:10pt;">1. The Equipment Record supports the need to maintain intelligence on individual pieces and groups of equipment. The Equipment record has been developed in AIntP-03 with general concepts flexible enough to be applied to a variety of objects. These objects may be military hardware such as tanks, planes, or ships, military associated items such as tents, radios, uniforms, small arms or horses. Physical structures such as buildings, bridges, bunkers, POL tanks etc. may also be categorised as Equipment in the database primarily for targeting purposes. These are generally referred to as Equipment Components and carry a unique Key that identifies their purpose. The equipment record provides a single focus for identifying the unique characteristics of equipment such as precise location, unique modifications or traits, physical design and dimensions, operational parameters, and assigned function.</span></p><p><span style="font-size:10pt;">2. The Equipment record is an entity in AIntP-03 and as such can exist without the organisation or facility to which the equipment belongs. Therefore equipment identified without recognition of the owning Organisation or Place, can be entered without requiring the creation of a bogus unidentified organisation or a placeholder facility. When one or both of those associations becomes known a relationship can be established from the equipment to the associated entity with all the detail remaining in place. On the other extreme it allows for multiple associations by other entities without affecting the information regarding the equipment itself. When a piece of equipment is associated with both a organisation and a facility, it is not necessary to determine ownership. The organisation can have a relationship with the equipment and the facility can also have a relationship to the same equipment record. Double counting can be avoided by specifying only one owner through the Association Values, while providing the capability for all the necessary relationships to be established. [AINTP-3]</span></p><p><span style="font-size:10pt;">3. Particular piece or group of equipment of the same type. A group of one or more pieces of equipment of the same type to which particular emitter, aircraft, or vessel belongs. A group of more than one type of equipment can be identified in Equipment Group to Equipment Group relationship. [AIDM Version 3/ BICC/ INTEL-FS Spiral 1]</span></p> |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_EUylxPlwEemoqLjBo08w4g |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 606/800 -->
<!-- Original visible header retained as metadata: NAF 4.0 L7 Information Model; Data Dictionary Version 1.1; original data dictionary page marker 558 / 1798 -->

If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

| Description | Equipment group to equipment group relationship. This link provides for the association of an Equipment to its subsystem Equipment or to other Equipment. ### 243. EssentialElementsOfInformation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 243 |
| Name | EssentialElementsOfInformation |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::IRMCM::IRM::ICP::EssentialElementsOfInformation |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | EEIs add detail to specific intelligence requirement and allow the production of a collection task list (CTL) based on an intelligence collection plan (ICP). EEIs could be related to several SIRs and should provide enough guidance to allow analysts to give a complete and satisfactory answer to each requirement. EEIs are the basis to create collection requirements and to establish relevant tasking and coordination with dedicated and non-dedicated collection capabilities or relevant agencies. [AJP-2.1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_uPYQ0J9SEeqGY6i_CL9eLQ |

### 245. Event

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 245 |
| Name | Event |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Action::Event::Event |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | Relevant event, (nuclear) attack, process, development, operation, program or activity that can be distinctly identified. The symbol for an Event object shall be automatically determined by the following attributes: - Event type - Event subtype. [AIDM Version 3/ BICC/ INTEL-FS Spiral 1]Event. A description of an incident or occurrence of some intelligence significance. An event may consist of a number of smaller events and, therefore, be capable of sub-division. [AINTP-3]An event is something that happens at a given place and time that is not a planned action (as seen from the planners perspective). TOPFAS Conceptual Data Model |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_l0tFUPokEemoqLjBo08w4g |

### 262. EventStatusReport

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 262 |
| Name | EventStatusReport |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Action::Event::EventStatusReport |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | Relevant event, (nuclear) attack, process, development, operation, program or activity that can be distinctly identified. The symbol for an Event object shall be automatically determined by the following attributes: - Event type - Event subtype. [AIDM Version 3/ BICC/ INTEL-FS Spiral 1]Event. A description of an incident or occurrence of some intelligence significance. An event may consist of a number of smaller events and, therefore, be capable of sub-division. [AINTP-3]An event is something that happens at a given place and time that is not a planned action (as seen from the planners perspective). TOPFAS Conceptual Data Model |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_l0u6gPokEemoqLjBo08w4g |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 661/800 -->
<!-- Original visible header retained as metadata: NAF 4.0 L7 Information Model; Data Dictionary Version 1.1; original data dictionary page marker 613 / 1798 -->

#### INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Action::Event::EventStatusReport - Owned Attributes

| Attribute Name | Type Name | Defining Class | Multiplicity | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Action | String | EventStatusReport | 0..1 |  |
| ActionStatus | ActionStatusType | EventStatusReport | 0..1 |  |
| Address | PhysicalAddress | StatusReport | 0..1 |  |
| AlternativelyKnownAs | BSOAlternativeIdentity | StatusReport | 0..* | Alternate Name for the BSO. If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| AnalystComment | String | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..1 | Comment on the product. The Comment field will identify analyst comments regarding the implications of the information and other assessments. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| AttackComplexity | AttackComplexityType | EventStatusReport | 0..1 |  |
| AttackLocations | EventLocationAttack | EventStatusReport | 0..1 |  |
| BPs | BusinessProcess | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..* |  |
| BSIIEAttributes | BattlespaceAttribute | StatusReport | 0..* |  |
| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |
| BattlespaceIIE | BattlespaceIIE | StatusReport | 1..1 |  |
| City | String | StatusReport | 0..1 |  |

<!-- Original visible header retained as metadata: NAF 4.0 L7 Information Model; Data Dictionary Version 1.1; original data dictionary page marker 614 / 1798 -->

### 266. Event_Equipment

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 266 |
| Name | Event_Equipment |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Action::Event::Event_Equipment |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | Equipment to event relationship. This link provides the association of a specific Event to Equipment. [AIDM Version 3/ BICC/ INTEL-FS Spiral 1]The link between the Event and an equipment is less common in the GMI context, but may be used, for example, to show the use by a terrorist of a particular weapon in a killing or terrorist act (Event), or as part of his normal modus-operandi. The use of a particular equipment (Water Cannon) in controlling a riot (Event) would be another example of such a linkage.Equipment that has caused or participated in an event of intelligence interest, or played a significant part in the initiation, control or termination of an event, should be associated with that event. The number of equipments, their status, condition and, if appropriate, their location should be recorded in the association record. The equipment record should contain all the fundamental equipment data. Again, it is worth considering the use of amplifying remarks. [AINTP-3] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_wP12YPpDEemoqLjBo08w4g |

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 267 |
| Name | Event_Event |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Action::Event::Event_Event |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | The association in the database of similar or related Events is very worthwhile, facilitating the future identification of trends and linking factors.Event. A description of an incident or occurrence of some intelligence significance. An event may consist of a number of smaller events and, therefore, be capable of sub-division. [AINTP-3] |

### 268. Event_Facility

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 268 |
| Name | Event_Facility |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Action::Event::Event_Facility |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | Event to place relationship. This link permits the association of an Event to a Place or Places within a given time-frame and identifies the reason for the association. [AIDM Version 3/ BICC/ INTEL-FS Spiral 1]An event that happened at or had an impact on a place or places should be associated in the intelligence record with that place or places. The event may also have affected the equipment and personnel at the place and, if so, should also be associated with the equipment(s) and individual personnel. At a minimum the following information should be recorded for the event: the event Key (unique reference), the event name (Hurricane "Mitch", "Shooting on the 25th April **", Exercise "Batch" etc.), the event type (disaster, political, terrorist etc), the specific type of incident (electronic jamming, death -military leader, drought etc.) and the timeframe of the event.The Event will normally have a geographic location and is likely to involve Places recognised in the intelligence record. These places should be associated with the Event. It may sometimes be necessary to expand on an association in the REMARKS Field.[AINTP-3] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_jpLbYPpEEemoqLjBo08w4g |

### 269. Event_Organisation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 269 |
| Name | Event_Organisation |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Action::Event::Event_Organisation |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | Organisations are likely to be affected by Events, particularly with a military context - exercises and operations (wars, uprisings and local conflicts). These organisations should be associated with the Event.An event that happened at or had an impact on an Organisation should be associated in the intelligence record with that Organisation. The event may also have affected the equipment and personnel at the Organisation and, if so, should also be associated with the equipment(s) and individual(s). As a minimum the following information should be recorded for the event: the event Key (unique reference), the event name (Bankruptcy, Hurricane "Mitch", "Shooting on the 25th April ", Exercise "Batch" etc.), the event type (disaster, political, terrorist etc), the specific type of incident (electronic jamming, death -military leader, drought etc.) and the timeframe of the event.[AINTP-3] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_-ac68PpDEemoqLjBo08w4g |

### 270. Event_Person

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 270 |
| Name | Event_Person |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Action::Event::Event_Person |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | The recording of the involvement of an individual or group of individuals in an Event may be appropriate. The event may be an exercise, operation, training programme, natural disaster, humanitarian operation etc. The nature of the involvement should be carefully explained (caused, involved, assisted etc.) and the time-span identified. [AINTP-3] |

| # | 271 |
| Name | Event_Unit |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Action::Event::Event_Unit |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | Organisations are likely to be affected by Events, particularly with a military context - exercises and operations (wars, uprisings and local conflicts). These organisations should be associated with the Event.An event that happened at or had an impact on an Organisation should be associated in the intelligence record with that Organisation. The event may also have affected the equipment and personnel at the Organisation and, if so, should also be associated with the equipment(s) and individual(s). As a minimum the following information should be recorded for the event: the event Key (unique reference), the event name (Bankruptcy, Hurricane "Mitch", "Shooting on the 25th April ", Exercise "Batch" etc.), the event type (disaster, political, terrorist etc), the specific type of incident (electronic jamming, death -military leader, drought etc.) and the timeframe of the event.[AINTP-3] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_mckJAPpEEemoqLjBo08w4g |

<!-- PDF_PAGE: 683/800 -->

| # | 272 |
| Name | EventsReport |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Staff::Product::APP-11::EventsReport::EventsReport |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | Message identifier (Name):EVENTREP (EVENTS REPORT) Related Documents:BI-SC REPORTING DIRECTIVE STANAG 2434 Purpose:THE EVENTREP IS USED TO PROVIDE NATO HQ AND NATIONS, THROUGH THE SC CHAIN OF COMMAND, INFORMATION ABOUT IMPORTANT EVENTS, TRENDS AND ACTIVITIES THAT DO NOT HAVE AN ELEMENT OF EXTREME URGENCY, BUT DO INFLUENCE PEACE SUPPORT OPERATIONS FORCE (PSOFOR) (E.G. IFOR, SFOR) OPERATIONS. Sponsor:SHAPE Notes:none Status:Published [APP-11-D-1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_SXhToKDHEeqz4qMNCq9PtQ |

| BPs | BusinessProcess | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..* |  |
| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | ProductIIE | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |

| Attribute Name | Type Name | Defining Class | Multiplicity | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Address | PhysicalAddress | StatusReport | 0..1 |  |
| AlternativelyKnownAs | BSOAlternativeIdentity | StatusReport | 0..* | Alternate Name for the BSO. If the name is reported in more than one language, the name in other languages except English will be tracked here. Also, nicknames, loose translations of the name, aliases, etc. will also be tracked here. The Alternate Name will follow the naming convention for the specific BSO, if identified. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| AnalystComment | String | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..1 | Comment on the product. The Comment field will identify analyst comments regarding the implications of the information and other assessments. [IFAS1-SOP] |
| BPs | BusinessProcess | IntelligenceInformationEntity | 0..* |  |
| BSIIEAttributes | BattlespaceAttribute | StatusReport | 0..* |  |
| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | StatusReport | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |
| BattlespaceIIE | BattlespaceIIE | StatusReport | 1..1 |  |

### 292. Facility_Equipment

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 292 |
| Name | Facility_Equipment |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Facility::Facility_Equipment |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | Although it is normal and best practice to associate equipment with an organisation for accounting purposes, equipment may have many associations. Certain equipment may, however, be owned by facilities. Typically such equipment is that which directly serves the facility, or is at a fixed site co-located within the boundary of the facility. Examples are: fixed site missiles, fire-fighting equipment and equipment components such as berthing facilities and cranes at port facilities, hard standings and aircraft shelters at airfields. Equally, equipment may be deployed to a place for exercise purposes, repair or training. When equipment is associated with a place, at a minimum the following information should be available within the record: the equipment key, the equipment common name or nomenclature, the type (tank, gun, armoured recovery vehicle etc) and the number, status and condition of the equipment held at the place. [AINTP-3]Place to equipment relationship.This link provides for the association of physical objects at a Place. It may reflect either Equipment or structural components.This link enables an analyst to account for Equipment unique to the mechanical or operational support of the Place as well as structural components (e.g. wharfs, piers, runways, bunkers, etc), which form an integral part of the Place, Installation or Facility. [AIDM Version 3/ BICC/ INTEL-FS Spiral 1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_ZlP4UPpEEemoqLjBo08w4g |

| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Facility::Facility_Facility |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | Relationship between places. This link provides for the association of Places with a functional relationship which is significant within the military, industrial or social infrastructure. It can provide the bases for transportation, distribution and communications Links formed between Populated Places, Installations, Facilities and Field Locations. [AIDM Version 3/ BICC/ INTEL-FS Spiral 1] |

### 296. Facility_Organisation

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 296 |
| Name | Facility_Organisation |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Facility::Facility_Organisation |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | Organisations that are permanently located or parented in peacetime at an installation or facility, such as a garrison or headquarters, should be associated to that facility in the intelligence record. Equally, Organisations that are known to deploy to an installation or facility should also be associated with that installation or facility. The specific minimum information that should be used to record the Organisation association is covered under that section. However, at a minimum, the Organisation key (unique reference), Organisation name, the role it has (civilian, military, diplomatic), the type of force to which it belongs (air, navy etc.), its mission (administrative, ordnance, missile etc.) and its allegiance should be recorded.The peacetime location of the Organisation at a permanent (garrison) site or HQs location should be recorded. The specific, minimum information that should be used to record an Installation/Facility is covered under that part of the BRs. However, at a minimum, the Place Key, Installation/Facility name, Functional Code and Location should be shown. [AINTP-3] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_KUec8PpEEemoqLjBo08w4g |

### 297. Facility_Person

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 297 |
| Name | Facility_Person |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Facility::Facility_Person |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | Person to place relationship. This link provides for the association of an individual to a specific Place, Installation or Facility. [AIDM Version 3/ BICC/ INTEL-FS Spiral 1]Individual(s) holding key post(s) at a place (installation/facility) should be recorded and associated with the place. At a minimum, the commending officer should be recorded and linked to the post he holds. The Biographic Record should show the Biographic Key assigned to the individual, the first, middle and last names, sex and the individuals allegiance. His professional status should be recorded, including the force (Army, Engineering Service, NATO) to which he is currently assigned. His role (Military Combat, Judiciary, Political Etc.); his native rank (Brigada, First Deputy Prime Minister, Oberleutnant etc.), the NATO equivalent rank and his position (Military Related, Commander / Officer Commanding, Industrial / Scientific, Mid Level Administrative Support, Military Related, Intelligence Officer etc) should also be recorded.In an ideal situation, an individual will belong to an organisation and be associated with the organisation as a manager, worker etc. He may also work at one or more places (usually where the organisation is located). It may be prudent to link the individual to one or more of these places, showing the place most commonly used as his working address. He may not be employed by the site (place), but by the occupying organisation or unit, so should be shown as located at the site for work (during peacetime/wartime etc?). The individual's location at a place may also include other reasons such as it being his home or for pleasure activities. It may sometimes be necessary to expand on an association in the REMARKS Field. [AINTP-3] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_qPwGwPpEEemoqLjBo08w4g |

### 298. Facility_Unit

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 298 |
| Name | Facility_Unit |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Facility::Facility_Unit |
| Type | `<AssociationClass>` |
| Description | Unit to place relationship. This link provides for association of a Unit with a garrison, deployed or transit location (Place). The relationship must reflect its condition, purpose and, where possible, the time-frame of the association. [AIDM Version 3/ BICC/ INTEL-FS Spiral 1]The peacetime location of the Organisation at a permanent (garrison) site or HQs location should be recorded. The specific, minimum information that should be used to record an Installation/Facility is covered under that part of the BRs. However, at a minimum, the Place Key, Installation/Facility name, Functional Code and Location should be shown. [AINTP-3] |

### 300. Feature

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| # | 300 |
| Name | Feature |
| Qualified Name | INTEL-FS Spiral 2::NATO::JISR::Battlespace::Object::Feature::Feature |
| Type | `<Class>` |
| Description | An Object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, or control features of military significance. Remarks:Features can be either natural features that may influence operations or artificial features representing administrative, political, or tactical constraints to be taken into account.Features are used to describe characteristics, phenomena, or other similar objects that are associated with a geographic location. These entities are of interest in the operational arena, and may be administratively defined or occur naturally. [MIM-5.1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_7BtBsDGrEeuZWPoXC6gYMw |

| Description | Message identifier (Name):FIRST HOSTILE ACT (FIRST HOSTILE ACT REPORT) Related Documents:BI-SC REPORTING DIRECTIVE, VOL. III STANAG 2434 Purpose:The FIRST HOSTILE ACT is used to rapidly provide major NATO commands with information on initial enemy/ opposition forces (OPFOR) hostile acts in order to enable major NATO commands to react as early as possible. Sponsor:SHAPE Notes:none Status:Published [APP-11-D-1] |
| Source URI | platform:/resource/INTEL-FS/INTEL-FS%20CIM%20RDM.emx#_8Mq0sKkQEeyPjPr1a0o5zw |

| BasicEncyclopediaNumber | BasicEncyclopediaNumberType | ProductIIE | 0..1 | The Basic Encyclopaedia (BE) Number is generated by a number of agencies involved in maintaining United States military databases and exists for a very large number of significant installations in the world. The AIntP-03 Key is based on a very similar data structure and can be easily converted to a BE Number. It is, however, recommended that when the BE Number for an Installation (or Facility) is known that it should be entered into the BE NUMBER Field and the BE NUMBER IS KEY Field switched to "Y" (Yes). Thus the BE Number would act as the Key for your record, improving the ability to interchange records without duplication or the creation on multiple, mismatched records on a single Place.[AINTP-3] |
